evaluations.query: Design RESTful API endpoints for a user management service
expected_behavior: Uses plural nouns for resources (/users, /orders)
Maps CRUD operations to correct HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
Returns appropriate status codes (200, 201, 204, 404, 422)
Implements cursor-based pagination for list endpoints

evaluations.query: Structure API endpoints for nested resources with filtering
expected_behavior: Uses hierarchical URLs for relationships (/users/123/orders)
Implements query parameter filtering for resource collections (?status=active)
Supports sort parameter for ordering results (?sort=-created_at)
Provides field selection for partial responses (?fields=id,name)

evaluations.query: Create OpenAPI specification for my API
expected_behavior: Uses OpenAPI 3.1 format with proper info section
Defines reusable schemas in components section
Documents all response codes including error responses
Includes rate limit headers and authentication schemes

evaluations.query: Implement URL path versioning for a FastAPI application
expected_behavior: Creates versioned directory structure (api/v1/, api/v2/)
Mounts version-specific routers with prefix (/api/v1, /api/v2)
Uses version-specific schemas while sharing service layer
Keeps business logic version-agnostic in services
Extracts version from X-API-Version header on incoming requests
Validates version against supported versions set
Defaults to latest stable version when header absent
Shows content negotiation alternative with Accept header

evaluations.query: Deprecate API v1 and sunset it in 6 months
expected_behavior: Adds Deprecation and Sunset headers to v1 responses
Includes Link header pointing to successor version
Distinguishes breaking vs non-breaking changes clearly
Shows version lifecycle: alpha -> beta -> stable -> deprecated -> sunset

evaluations.query: Implement RFC 9457 Problem Details for API errors
expected_behavior: Creates ProblemException base class with type, title, status, detail, instance
Returns application/problem+json media type in the Content-Type response header
Supports extension members for additional context
Registers FastAPI exception handler for ProblemException

evaluations.query: Handle validation errors with field-level details
expected_behavior: Returns 422 with field-level error array
Maps Pydantic RequestValidationError to Problem Details format
Includes field path, error code, and human-readable message
Uses consistent error structure across all validation failures

evaluations.query: Create a centralized error type registry for my API
expected_behavior: Defines problem type URIs for each error category
Maps standard HTTP status codes to problem types
Shows Python and TypeScript client handling examples
Includes specific exception subclasses for each problem type

evaluations.query: Set up a GraphQL API with Strawberry and FastAPI that has authentication, authorization, and real-time subscriptions for user updates
expected_behavior: Uses Strawberry permission classes like IsAuthenticated for field-level authorization
Integrates with FastAPI via GraphQLRouter with a context getter for dependency injection
Implements subscriptions with Redis PubSub for horizontal scaling of real-time updates
Uses opaque strawberry.ID instead of exposing internal auto-increment integer IDs

evaluations.query: My GraphQL resolvers are making N+1 database queries when fetching posts with their authors. How do I fix this with Strawberry?
expected_behavior: Uses DataLoader with batch_load function to batch nested resolver database queries
Defines code-first types with strawberry.type decorator for type-safe schema generation
Uses Private field annotation to hide sensitive data like password hashes from schema
Implements union types for mutation error handling with success and error result variants

evaluations.query: I need to set up gRPC for internal microservice communication between our user service and order service with proper error handling
expected_behavior: Defines protobuf service with proto3 syntax including message types and enums
Implements async server servicer with context.abort for proper gRPC error codes
Configures client with keepalive options and timeout on every call
Maps gRPC status codes like NOT_FOUND and INVALID_ARGUMENT to domain exceptions

evaluations.query: Add server streaming and bidirectional streaming to my gRPC service with authentication interceptors and retry logic
expected_behavior: Checks context.is_active before yielding each item in server streaming responses
Implements auth interceptor with metadata extraction and public method allowlist
Uses client retry interceptor with exponential backoff for UNAVAILABLE and DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
Supports bidirectional streaming with async request iterator processing

evaluations.query: I need to integrate WhatsApp and Telegram messaging into our platform with webhook endpoints. How do I handle webhook security and message delivery?
expected_behavior: Verifies webhook signatures before processing any incoming messages from platforms
Acknowledges webhook immediately with 200 OK and processes messages asynchronously via queue
Stores message IDs and deduplicates on retry to prevent duplicate message processing
Rate-limits outgoing messages according to each platform's specific rate limits

evaluations.query: Set up Payload CMS 3.0 collections for a blog with proper access control and content management in our Next.js project
expected_behavior: Defines collection config with role-based access control functions for read create update delete
Sets overrideAccess to false in API routes serving user requests to enforce access control
Keeps hooks thin for validation and transformation only and delegates logic to services
Uses S3-compatible storage adapter for file uploads instead of storing files in database

evaluations.query: Create a Next.js SSE endpoint for streaming LLM token responses to the browser with keepalive and reconnection support
expected_behavior: Sets Content-Type to text/event-stream with Cache-Control no-cache headers
Implements keepalive comment every thirty seconds to prevent connection timeout
Handles client disconnect via abort signal to clean up resources properly
Builds reconnecting client with exponential backoff from one second to thirty seconds maximum

evaluations.query: Implement a WebSocket server for real-time chat with heartbeat, message validation, and proper async generator cleanup for LLM streaming
expected_behavior: Implements heartbeat ping every thirty seconds and clears interval on connection close
Validates message structure before broadcasting to prevent raw unvalidated data relay
Uses aclosing context manager for guaranteed async generator cleanup on external streams
Broadcasts only to other connected clients with readyState check before sending
